title: Cupcake Tiered Display Stand
summary: A four-tier stepped display stand built from stacked square bases and overhanging tread boards — a party or bakery-counter piece, no fasteners visible from the front.
description:
This stand is four square box "bases" of decreasing size, glued and stacked one on top of the next, with a flat "tread" board glued and nailed over each step to create the display surface. Attach the bases together first, checking each for square, before stacking begins — a base built out of square multiplies into a visibly crooked stack by the time you reach the top tier.

Stack largest to smallest, gluing each base's top edge before setting the next one down, and keep the back and sides flush and lined up as you go — the back stays a flat vertical line all the way up, which is what lets the stand sit flush against a wall or table backdrop.

The treads overhang the front and sides of each base evenly and get glued and nailed from the top, then reinforced with nails driven up from behind so no fastener shows on the display surface. An optional 1x2 brace along the back adds stiffness if the stand will travel to events rather than sit in one place.

steps.title: Build the bases
steps.body: Attach each base's sides together with three 1-1/4" brad nails and glue per corner. Adjust each for square before moving to the next.

steps.title: Stack the bases
steps.body: Apply glue to the top edges of the largest base and set the next-largest base on top. Repeat up through all four bases, keeping the backs and sides flush and lined up as you stack.

steps.title: Attach the treads
steps.body: Apply glue to the back edges of the treads, then set them on top of each base with even overhangs on the front and sides, flush to the back. Attach with 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Reinforce and finish
steps.body: From the back, drive additional brad nails up into the treads for extra strength — no fasteners are visible on the display surface. Add an optional 1x2 back brace if the stand will travel. Sand, fill any holes, and stain.
